title: AI Engineering
description: Build production-grade applications on top of foundation models. You will progress through prompt engineering fundamentals, embeddings and vector stores, retrieval-augmented generation, evaluations and golden sets, fine-tuning basics, safety and alignment fundamentals, deployment and cost engineering, and agentic patterns. By the end you will be able to ship an LLM-backed feature with confidence in its correctness, cost, and safety profile.

modules.title: Prompt Engineering Fundamentals
modules.description: Structure prompts for clarity, controllability, and resilience. Roles, delimiters, few-shot examples, and chain-of-thought guidance.
objectives: Explain the difference between zero-shot, few-shot, and chain-of-thought prompts
Author a prompt with explicit role, scope, and refusal rules
Identify a vague prompt and rewrite it for testability
Compare outputs from two prompt variants on the same input

modules.title: Embeddings and Vector Stores
modules.description: Turn text into vectors and search by semantic similarity. Cosine vs dot-product, chunking strategy, and index choice.
objectives: Explain how embedding models map text into a vector space
Embed a small corpus and query it with cosine similarity
Choose a chunk size and overlap appropriate for a given document type
Compare HNSW, IVF, and flat indexes on recall and latency

modules.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
modules.description: Stitch retrieval and generation into a single pipeline. Query rewriting, top-k tuning, and answer grounding.
objectives: Diagram a RAG pipeline from query through retrieval through generation
Implement query rewriting that improves retrieval recall
Detect and reduce hallucinated citations in a RAG answer
Tune top-k and reranker thresholds on a known evaluation set

modules.title: Evaluations and Golden Sets
modules.description: Quantify LLM behavior with deterministic and LLM-as-judge evals. Build a golden set and a regression harness.
objectives: Define golden-set design criteria for an LLM feature
Compare deterministic eval rubrics with LLM-as-judge rubrics
Catch a model-regression with a CI-runnable eval suite
Estimate variance and confidence intervals on small eval sets

modules.title: Fine-Tuning Basics
modules.description: When to fine-tune versus when to prompt or retrieve. Supervised fine-tuning, LoRA adapters, and data curation.
objectives: List signals that justify fine-tuning over prompting or retrieval
Curate a 200-example supervised dataset with quality gates
Compare full fine-tuning with LoRA on cost and accuracy
Detect overfitting on a held-out validation slice

modules.title: Safety and Alignment Fundamentals
modules.description: Refusal patterns, jailbreak red-teaming, and content-policy enforcement. Establish a safety harness before launch.
objectives: Define a refusal taxonomy for an LLM feature
Run a red-team prompt-injection battery and triage the results
Add a content-policy filter on inputs and outputs
Audit a safety incident and update the harness

modules.title: Deployment and Cost Engineering
modules.description: Ship LLM workloads with predictable cost and latency. Caching, batching, model routing, and observability.
objectives: Calculate the unit cost of an LLM-backed feature per active user
Implement a response cache with semantic-key invalidation
Route easy requests to a cheaper model and hard ones to a stronger model
Instrument token-cost and latency dashboards with alerts

modules.title: Agentic Patterns
modules.description: Tool-use, planning, and reflection. Build a multi-step agent with explicit budgets and traceable reasoning.
objectives: Compare ReAct, plan-then-act, and tree-of-thought agent loops
Implement a tool-using agent with a token and call budget
Add a self-reflection step that rejects low-confidence plans
Trace an agent run end-to-end for postmortem analysis

modules.title: Capstone: Ship an LLM Feature
modules.description: Take a real product use case from prompt design through evals to deployment with cost and safety dashboards.
objectives: Pick a use case and write the success criteria and golden set
Implement RAG, evals, safety filter, and cost ceiling
Run a pre-launch eval gate that blocks regressions
Demonstrate the feature working under a hostile prompt battery
